mappings.rationale: Directly covers the applications of mathematics to physical, biological, and social sciences, which is the central subject of this section.
Section D covers statistical sciences including mathematical statistics, stochastic processes, and their applications across disciplines.
Probability underpins statistical inference (section D) and mathematical optimization (section E), as well as actuarial science.
Section E.1 covers the theory of games as analysis of strategic features of conflict situations, which is precisely game theory.
Section G covers computer science including algorithms, theory of computation, computer architecture, programming languages, and artificial intelligence.
Section G.6 specifically lists artificial intelligence as a branch of computer science within applied mathematics.
Section G covers computer science including computer architecture and programming, and the section references computational aids and digital devices.
Section G covers computation theory and computer architecture, topics deeply intertwined with the historical development of computing.
Big data relates to statistical sciences (section D) and computer science (section G), especially numeric computation and data analysis.
Network theory connects to cybernetics (E.3), information theory (F), and computational methods (G) as a mathematical application.
Section F.4 specifically names cryptography as an application of information theory.
Section F covers information theory including its origins, central problems, principles, and applications to cryptography and linguistics.
Chaos theory applies mathematical analysis to physical systems, relevant to section H on mathematical aspects of physical theories and control theory (E.4).
Complexity science connects to cybernetics (E.3), control theory (E.4), and computational methods (G) as applied mathematical frameworks.
Robotics draws on cybernetics (E.3), control theory (E.4), and artificial intelligence (G.6), which are all covered in this section.
Mathematical finance applies probability, statistics, and optimization theory (sections D and E) to financial systems.
Section H.6 covers relativity theory as a mathematical framework involving four-dimensional spacetime continuum.
Section H.8 covers quantum mechanics as a mathematical framework for physical theories.
Section H.2 specifically covers fluid mechanics as a mathematical aspect of physical theories.
Fractals represent applications of mathematical analysis to geometry and physical phenomena, connecting to sections D and H.
Group theory and symmetry underpin the mathematical aspects of physical theories in section H, especially mechanics and quantum mechanics.
Measurement theory connects to statistical sciences (section D) and the mathematical methods applied to empirical observations.
Biometrics applies statistical methods (section D) and computational techniques (section G) to biological measurement data.
Choice theory connects to game theory (E.1) and linear/nonlinear programming (E.2) as mathematical optimization applied to decision-making.
